lorehq

─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos

Active Work Architecture
─[ Multi-Tier Production Readiness ]─ ┐

Multi-Tier Production Readiness

Overview

This roadmap now has a narrower role: prepare and validate team-scale Lore operation after standalone go-live is stabilized.

Immediate go-live positioning work is tracked in a separate roadmap:

  • Standalone Go-Live Positioning

The sequencing is intentional: standalone trust and adoption first, multi-tier scale and governance second.

Entry Gate

Start this roadmap in earnest when all three are true:

  1. Standalone go-live trust artifacts are published (governance + security + release baseline)
  2. Standalone adoption evidence shows traction beyond single-user usage
  3. Standalone setup window has completed (private hardening phase closed and visibility/public posture intentionally decided)

Phases

Phase 0: Multi-Tier Pilot Readiness (Deferred)

Status: Planned

  • Complete remaining docs audience separation and canonical ownership mapping
  • Finalize contribution/sync maintainer decision ergonomics
  • Confirm pilot preflight checks remain green on main
  • Validate deterministic agent generation assumptions across identical skill sets
  • Prepare multi-tier-specific adoption framing and enterprise evaluation readiness

Plans:

  • Documentation Restructuring
  • Contribution and Sync Hardening
  • Evidence and Validation
  • Ecosystem Readiness and Hardening

Phase 1: 2-Tier Pilot (3-5 days)

Status: Planned

  • Run controlled team -> instance pilot with 1-2 participants
  • Capture sync/conflict reliability and operator friction
  • Validate PR-first contribution flow under daily usage
  • Log bounded direct-mode experiments and rollback outcomes

Plans:

  • Live Test Protocol
  • Evidence and Validation

Phase 2: Post-Pilot Remediation

Status: Planned

  • Resolve pilot blockers and high-friction workflows
  • Improve conflict ergonomics and recovery guidance
  • Close high-impact docs drift discovered during pilot

Plans:

  • Documentation Restructuring
  • Contribution and Sync Hardening

Phase 3: 3-Tier Live Test (~1 week)

Status: Planned

  • Run org -> team -> instance controlled live test
  • Verify zero silent overwrites and acceptable conflict rate
  • Validate cross-tier contribution and propagation cadence
  • Capture cross-member acceleration examples

Plans:

  • Live Test Protocol

Phase 4: Technical Modernization

Status: Planned

  • Reduce maintenance debt after live validation
  • Consolidate duplicated sync/contribution implementations
  • Expand static analysis and parsing robustness where justified
  • Evaluate skill maturity/version tooling and promotion automation

Plans:

  • Technical Modernization

Success Criteria

2-Tier Gate

  • One full pilot cycle completes: deploy -> sync -> contribute -> merge -> sync back
  • Zero data loss and zero silent overwrite incidents

3-Tier Gate

  • One full live-test week completes successfully
  • At least 3 skills are contributed and merged across tiers

Operational Confidence

  • Sync and contribution workflows remain predictable under concurrent real usage
  • High-severity pilot findings are resolved before broader multi-tier promotion

Risks and Mitigations

RiskMitigation
Starting multi-tier before standalone trust is establishedEnforce entry gate tied to standalone roadmap outcomes
Pilot reveals high conflict burdenPause scale-up and prioritize remediation before 3-tier testing
Multi-tier complexity dilutes launch narrativeKeep this roadmap intentionally separate from go-live roadmap
